advantages: Explicit dtypes at load prevent silent type errors (IDs parsed as floats) and cut memory versus default float64/object inference.
Parquet column pruning reads only needed fields from disk, speeding I/O on wide tables.
Loading with `parse_dates` and `na_values` centralizes parsing rules once instead of repeating fixes in cleaning code.
Memory audits immediately after load surface bottlenecks before expensive joins or model training.
disadvantages: CSV has no embedded schema—wrong assumptions about dtypes require a second full read after discovery.
Over-aggressive downcasting (int8 for IDs) can overflow or lose precision if value ranges are mis-estimated.
JSON normalization can explode column count and memory when nested structures are deeply variable.
Loading entire multi-GB files on a laptop may fail regardless of dtype tricks—requires chunked reads or out-of-core tools.
